title: Boat Rental in Sharm El Sheikh: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Taking to the Red Sea from Sharm El Sheikh offers an extraordinary day on the water. Departing from the marina at Naama Bay, you can expect to pay between 1,500 and 12,000 EGP for a half-day charter. BoatHire24 connects you with local skippers and owners who offer verified trips.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Sharm El Sheikh?

Renting a boat in Sharm El Sheikh costs from 1,500 EGP for a shared tour to 12,000 EGP for a full-day crewed yacht. Prices vary by boat size, duration and season. For a private half-day on a small motorboat, expect around 3,000 to 5,000 EGP. A luxury catamaran for a full day with lunch and drinks typically costs 8,000 to 12,000 EGP. Checking live availability on day charters in Sharm El Sheikh helps you compare operators and find the best deal.

  • Shared snorkelling tour: from 1,500 EGP per person for a half-day.
  • Small motorboat (private): 3,000, 5,000 EGP for a half-day.
  • Catamaran (full-day with crew): 8,000, 12,000 EGP for up to 12 guests.
  • Luxury motor yacht (full-day): 10,000, 15,000 EGP for larger groups.

Where to set off in Sharm El Sheikh

The main departure point is the Marina at Naama Bay, but there are several other harbours and bays from which to start your excursion. Sharm el-Maya Bay offers a quieter launch point, while Sharks Bay is popular for its nearby reefs. For longer trips, boats often leave from the port at Ras Nasrani. The Strait of Tiran is a short 15 nautical mile run from Naama Bay, and the island of Tiran itself offers stunning anchorages. Further south, the Ras Mohammed National Park is a 20-nautical-mile trip and is home to some of the Red Sea's finest dive sites.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (e.g. Olympic 490), ideal for couples or small groups of up to 4, quick trips to nearby reefs, from 3,000 EGP half-day. See details on the Olympic 490 charter page for specs and availability.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42), perfect for families or groups of 8, 12, offers stability and space, from 8,000 EGP full-day.
  3. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), for up to 10 guests, includes crew and amenities, from 10,000 EGP full-day.
  4. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), fast and nimble for up to 6, great for water sports and exploring, from 2,500 EGP half-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Sharm El Sheikh?

No licence is typically required for bareboat charters in Egypt, but you must be at least 18 years old and the operator confirms exact requirements. Most holidaymakers opt for a skippered charter, which is included in the rental price. For a self-drive experience, the operator will check your experience and may require a brief check-out. It is always best to ask the boat owner on the day of booking.

For a quieter day on the water, avoid the weekend (Friday and Saturday) and choose a morning departure between May and September, when the wind is usually lighter.

When is the best season for boating in Sharm El Sheikh?

The best season runs from March to November, with high season in April, May, September and October. Summer months (June to August) can be very hot but offer calm seas. Winter (December to February) sees cooler temperatures and stronger winds, particularly the north-westerly 'Shamal', which can make the Red Sea choppier. For 2026, plan your trip between March and November for the most pleasant conditions.

At a glance

  • Price band: 1,500, 15,000 EGP
  • Peak months: April, May, September, October
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Maximum guests: up to 12 on most boats
  • Currency: EGP (Egyptian Pound)

Questions about boat rental in Sharm El Sheikh

Can I rent a boat for a few hours only?

Yes, many operators offer half-day charters of four or five hours, which are ideal for snorkelling trips to Ras Mohammed or the Strait of Tiran.

What is included in the rental price?

Usually the price includes the skipper, fuel, snorkelling equipment and soft drinks; lunch is often extra unless noted, so check the listing.

How many people can fit on a motorboat?

Small motorboats typically take up to six passengers, while larger yachts and catamarans can accommodate twelve or more guests comfortably.

Do I need to tip the crew?

Tipping is customary in Egypt; a 10, 15% gratuity of the charter fee is standard for good service.

Whether you are a visitor looking to explore the Red Sea or a boat owner with a vessel at Marina Naama Bay, BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Sharm El Sheikh or list your own. For the widest choice of skippered and bareboat options, visit the BoatHire24 marketplace and find your perfect day on the water. Boat owners can also create a profile and start earning from their boat.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Sharm El Sheikh and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
